title: Classic Pancakes
description: Fluffy, golden pancakes perfect for a weekend breakfast.

steps.text: Whisk flour, baking powder, and sugar in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, beat eggs, milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract.
Pour wet ingredients into dry and stir until just combined — small lumps are fine.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and lightly grease it.
Pour ¼ cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on top, then flip and cook 1 minute more.

title: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: A Roman classic — just pasta, garlic, olive oil, and a little patience.

steps.text: Cook pasta in heavily salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Thinly slice garlic. Warm olive oil in a large pan over low heat and gently fry garlic until golden, about 4 minutes.
Add drained pasta and a splash of pasta water. Toss vigorously over medium heat until the sauce emulsifies and coats every strand.

title: Honey Garlic Chicken
description: Sticky, sweet-savory chicken that comes together in one pan in under 25 minutes.

steps.text: Pound chicken breasts to an even thickness and season with salt and pepper.
Sear in olive oil over high heat, 4 minutes each side. Set aside.
In the same pan, fry minced garlic 30 seconds, then add honey and 2 tbsp water. Stir and simmer 2 minutes.
Return chicken to pan and spoon glaze over it. Cook 2 more minutes until coated and sticky.

title: Lemon Butter Salmon
description: Pan-seared salmon finished in a bright lemon-butter sauce — on the table in 15 minutes.

steps.text: Pat salmon dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
Melt half the butter in a hot pan. Sear salmon skin-side up for 4 minutes, then flip and cook 3 minutes more.
Remove salmon. Add remaining butter, minced garlic, and lemon juice to the pan.
Swirl sauce and pour over salmon to serve.

title: Chocolate Lava Cake
description: Decadent individual chocolate cakes with a molten centre — best served immediately.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 220 °C. Butter and flour 4 ramekins.
Melt chocolate and butter together over a bain-marie. Cool slightly.
Whisk eggs and sugar until pale and thick. Fold in chocolate mixture, then flour.
Divide batter among ramekins and bake 10–12 minutes until edges are set but centres still wobble.
Rest 1 minute, then invert onto plates and serve immediately.

title: Creamy Pasta Carbonara
description: The authentic Roman carbonara — no cream, just eggs, cheese, and technique.

steps.text: Cook pasta in well-salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Whisk eggs with grated cheddar until smooth.
Drain pasta and immediately toss with olive oil. Remove from heat.
Pour egg mixture over hot pasta, adding pasta water a tablespoon at a time, tossing constantly until silky.

title: Greek Yogurt Honey Bowl
description: A simple, protein-rich breakfast or snack bowl ready in 2 minutes.

steps.text: Spoon Greek yogurt into a bowl.
Drizzle honey over the top.
Squeeze a little fresh lemon juice over and serve immediately.

title: Cheesy Scrambled Eggs
description: Slow-cooked, creamy scrambled eggs with melted cheddar — the best way to start any day.

steps.text: Whisk eggs with milk and a pinch of salt until fully combined.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan over the lowest heat setting.
Add egg mixture and stir slowly and continuously with a spatula, pulling from the edges, for 5–7 minutes.
Just before the eggs fully set, stir in grated cheddar and remove from heat. Residual heat will finish cooking them.

title: Dark Chocolate Mousse
description: Light, airy chocolate mousse made with just four ingredients. Chill for at least 2 hours.

steps.text: Melt chocolate over a bain-marie and set aside to cool to room temperature.
Whip heavy cream to soft peaks. Whip egg whites with sugar to stiff peaks.
Fold cream into chocolate, then gently fold in egg whites in two additions.
Divide into 4 glasses and refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving.

title: Vanilla Panna Cotta
description: Silky Italian panna cotta with a subtle vanilla flavour and honey glaze.

steps.text: Combine cream, milk, sugar, and vanilla in a saucepan. Heat until steaming, then remove from heat.
Bloom 2 tsp gelatine in 2 tbsp cold water for 5 minutes, then stir into the warm cream until dissolved.
Pour into 4 ramekins and refrigerate for at least 4 hours until set.
To serve, run a knife around the edge, invert onto a plate, and drizzle with honey.

title: Honey Lemon Glazed Salmon
description: Bright and sticky salmon that works equally well for a weeknight dinner or a dinner party.

steps.text: Mix honey, lemon juice, and minced garlic into a glaze.
Season salmon fillets and brush generously with half the glaze.
Heat olive oil in an oven-proof pan. Sear salmon 2 minutes each side.
Brush remaining glaze over the top and finish in a 200 °C oven for 6 minutes.

title: Chicken Rice Bowl
description: A clean, high-protein bowl with garlicky chicken over fragrant brown rice.

steps.text: Cook brown rice according to package directions. Fluff and set aside.
Dice chicken and season with salt and pepper.
Sauté garlic in olive oil for 30 seconds, add chicken and cook through, about 8 minutes.
Dice tomatoes and add to pan for the last 2 minutes.
Serve chicken mixture over rice.

title: Creamy Tomato Pasta
description: A quick and comforting pasta sauce that comes together in the time the pasta takes to cook.

steps.text: Cook pasta in salted water until al dente.
Sauté garlic in olive oil until soft. Add chopped tomatoes and simmer 10 minutes.
Stir in heavy cream and simmer 3 more minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
Toss drained pasta through the sauce and serve immediately.

title: Butter Cake
description: A classic, moist butter cake that stays fresh for days — if it lasts that long.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 175 °C. Grease and line a 20 cm cake tin.
Beat butter and sugar until light and fluffy, about 5 minutes.
Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix in vanilla.
Sift in flour and baking powder. Fold in with milk until smooth — do not over-mix.
Pour into tin and bake 45–50 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes before turning out.

title: Yogurt Honey Cake
description: A moist, lightly sweet cake using Greek yogurt for a tender crumb and subtle tang.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 180 °C. Grease a loaf tin.
Whisk eggs, yogurt, honey, and vanilla until smooth.
Fold in flour and baking powder until just combined.
Pour into tin and bake 40–45 minutes. Cool completely before slicing.

title: Salmon Pasta
description: Elegant yet weeknight-easy — flaked salmon with a light lemon cream sauce.

steps.text: Cook pasta until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water.
Pan-fry salmon fillets in olive oil, 3–4 minutes each side. Flake into large chunks.
In the same pan, sauté garlic 30 seconds. Add cream and lemon juice, simmer 2 minutes.
Toss pasta and salmon in the sauce, adding pasta water to loosen if needed.

title: Classic Crêpes
description: Thin French crêpes suitable for sweet or savoury fillings.

steps.text: Blend flour, eggs, milk, melted butter, sugar, and vanilla into a smooth batter.
Rest batter in the fridge for 30 minutes.
Heat a lightly buttered 20 cm pan over medium-high heat. Pour in a thin layer of batter, swirling to coat.
Cook 1 minute until edges curl, then flip and cook 30 seconds more. Stack and keep warm.

title: Garlic Butter Rice
description: Simple but deeply flavourful brown rice cooked with garlic and finished with butter.

steps.text: Rinse rice until water runs clear.
Sauté minced garlic in olive oil over medium heat for 1 minute.
Add rice and toast 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add 600 ml water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover and cook 40 minutes.
Fluff rice and stir through butter until melted.

title: Lemon Yogurt Dressing
description: A tangy, creamy dressing for grain bowls, salads, or grilled vegetables.

steps.text: Zest the lemon, then juice it.
Whisk together yogurt, lemon juice and zest, honey, olive oil, and minced garlic.
Season with salt and pepper. Thin with a splash of water if needed.
Refrigerate up to 5 days in a sealed jar.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Thick, chewy cookies with crisp edges and pools of dark chocolate throughout.

steps.text: Beat butter and sugar until pale and fluffy.
Add eggs and vanilla and beat until combined.
Fold in flour, then stir in chopped dark chocolate.
Scoop dough into balls and chill 30 minutes.
Bake at 180 °C for 11–13 minutes. Cool on tray — they firm up as they cool.
